description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.title: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 1 (moderate): count drift between 70 (dome-claimed) and 72 (catalog) unreconciled across prose
issues.description: 129 hardcoded mentions of 70 vs 15 of 72 across rendered HTML. The Overview verdict-bars has a reconciling sentence (70+2-collision=72) but hundreds of prose mentions say "Of the 70 WINs reviewed", "all 70 WINs", etc. without distinguishing dome headline vs review denominator. Curmudgeon recommends: pick one approach — (a) name the denominator at each mention, or (b) recompute prose against {{TOTAL_WINS}}=72 everywhere and reframe the reconciling sentence. Either path eliminates the "your own counts disagree" attack.
issues.routing_reason: Wide-ranging prose fix (129 mentions across rendered HTML) — requires systematic search-and-replace with editorial decisions on denominator naming. Route to analyst for bundle with any 72/70 reconciliation work.

issues.title: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 4 (minor): Timestamp Error tab (pos 8) narratively orphaned between Kill Shots and Predictions
issues.description: Tab order: Overview→Eval Guide→Model→Self-Contradictions→70 Wins→Live Power→Kill Shots→Timestamp Error→Predictions→Falsify→AI→Refs. Timestamp Error tab (methodology finding, adjacent to Code Analysis and AI Directives) sits between Kill Shots and Predictions, interrupting the test-cadence. Curmudgeon recommends moving Timestamp Error adjacent to Self-Contradictions tab or combining with Code Analysis into "Methodology Audit." Counter-case: tab is the OTS canary so visibility may justify its standalone position.
issues.routing_reason: Editorial/structural tab-ordering change. Analyst should evaluate whether canary visibility justifies current position vs moving adjacent to methodology-audit tabs.

issues.title: CROSSREF c3 hole 5 (moderate): imprecise hrefs for Section 1.8 and Section 6.6 (5 fixable instances)
issues.description: 5 instances where a generic part-level anchor (#part1 or #p6-66-5) is used where a specific section anchor exists: 3x Section 1.8 → #section-1-8; 1x full-source-code analysis (Section 6.6) → #p6-66-5; 1x Section 6.2 → #p6-62-1. These are precision improvements, not broken links. Decider c2 deferred once; curmudgeon recommendation remains. Analyst can patch these in a single pass against sections.json.
issues.routing_reason: Batch of 5 find/replace edits in sections.json. Analyst can apply in single EXP.

issues.title: COMPLETENESS c3 hole 1 (critical): Part 2b §2b.1 stratification paragraph hard-coded WIN-ID lists do not match wins.json
issues.description: The stratification paragraph in Part 2b §2b.1 ("Stratifying the unmonitored WINs") contains hard-coded WIN-ID lists and counts ("Two are theological assertions (WIN-032, WIN-034)") that no longer match wins.json. WIN-034 is now Self-Contradicted, not Unfalsifiable/theological. The paragraph also has hard-coded monitoring=none counts. Fix: either template the WIN-ID lists with build-time-computed placeholders, or regenerate the lists from wins.json. This is a wide-ranging rewrite of the stratification paragraph — route to analyst.
issues.routing_reason: Complex rewrite: stratification paragraph needs WIN-ID lists regenerated from wins.json or templated.

issues.title: COMPLETENESS c3 hole 2 (major): Part 2b §2b.1 contrast paragraph counts do not match current wins.json data
issues.description: Contrast paragraph in §2b.1 says "the dome's 14 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), seven have no monitoring at all — but the other seven (WIN-002/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring." Current wins.json has 20 Self-Contradicted WINs (not 14). The monitoring=none/hardcoded split within that set also needs recomputation. Fix: recalculate from wins.json and rewrite the sentence with correct counts and ID lists.
issues.location: data/sections.json:part2b.html §2b.1 contrast paragraph ("The contrast with the hardcoded WINs")
issues.routing_reason: Requires recomputing SC counts from wins.json and rewriting.

issues.title: COMPLETENESS c3 hole 3 (major): WIN-067 listed in §2b.1 monitoring=none Self-Contradicted set but cited in §2b.2 as having monitoring
issues.description: Section §2b.1 line 13 lists WIN-067 in the monitoring=none Self-Contradicted set ("WIN-067 gravity"). Section §2b.2 cites WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity) as the canonical example of "silent substitution" — which implies it has monitoring. These two mentions are internally inconsistent. Fix: remove WIN-067 from the §2b.1 monitoring=none list (it belongs in the hardcoded/monitoring category, not monitoring=none). §2b.2 and §2b.4 QUAD_FAIL citations are correct and need no change.
issues.routing_reason: Requires verifying WIN-067 monitoring tag and removing from the monitoring=none list.

issues.title: COMPLETENESS c3 hole 4 (moderate): "39 monitoring domains" count in §2b.1 needs versioned anchoring or re-verification
issues.description: Section §2b.1 cites "39 monitoring domains" anchored to commit 8e4bf9b (V51.0, 2026-04-05). Current dome live-power dashboard shows "20-Domain Convergence Dashboard" (different metric). The 39-domain count is the underlying monitor.py count vs the 20-domain dashboard display. Fix: either re-verify against current monitor.py and update the count, or explicitly mark "as of V51.0 commit 8e4bf9b, 2026-04-05" so readers can distinguish.
issues.routing_reason: Needs poller fresh clone verification or prose anchoring.

issues.title: NARRATIVE c2 hole 3 (moderate): stance statement appears before document title — reframe needed
issues.description: The Overview's stance statement ("The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none...") is the first thing readers see — before the title, before the verdict bars. A dome defender can attack this as question-begging. Options: (a) separate stance from findings visually by moving it below the title with a clear "Editorial stance" label; (b) reframe it as a transparency disclosure rather than the document's first proposition; (c) move it to the bottom. Affects generate-html.js or Overview section prose.
issues.location: Overview tab: ds-stance-statement block, position relative to title
issues.routing_reason: Structural UX decision requiring analyst judgment on best approach.

issues.title: NARRATIVE c2 hole 4 (minor): Timestamp Error tab narratively orphaned from surrounding argument flow
issues.description: The tab order places Timestamp Error (tab 8) between Kill Shots and Predictions Analysis. Kill Shots and Predictions Analysis are thematically connected (prediction evaluation). Timestamp Error is a dome-internal methodology critique, more naturally adjacent to Self-Contradictions / Part 2b code analysis. Either move Timestamp Error adjacent to Part 2b, or add a brief narrative bridge in the Kill Shots outro pointing to Timestamp Error as the methodology-audit companion.
issues.routing_reason: Low-impact UX improvement. Analyst writes bridge text or recommends tab reorder.

issues.title: WIN-004.c7 minor 2 (minor): PRED-013 math stagnated 4 cycles — close or integrate into detail_extra
issues.description: WIN-004.detail_extra remains null for the 4th consecutive review cycle (c4→c5→c6→c7 unaddressed). The PRED-013 feasibility math (linear 2055 extrapolation = 74.4°; 2.92× current rate required for lower bound; 5.45× for upper bound) has been recommended for integration since c4. Options: (a) integrate as 3-line detail_extra block; (b) explicitly mark detail_extra as intentionally null and close this carry-over. Curmudgeon recommends (a) with the exact text: "Linear extrapolation: 74.4° in 2055. PRED-013 lower bound: 2.92× current rate required. Upper bound: 5.45× current rate required."
issues.routing_reason: Analyst decides: integrate 3-line math or explicitly close as wontfix.
